title: Vikunja Reminder Agent — rules.json
description: Configures notification provider instances and routing rules for the Vikunja Reminder Agent.

providers.description: Named provider instances. Each key is a unique instance name you choose (e.g. 'discord-personal', 'slack-work'). Referenced by name in rule 'providers' arrays.

rules.description: Ordered list of routing rules. Evaluated top-to-bottom for every reminder that fires.

ProviderInstance.description: A configured instance of a notification plugin.

plugin.description: Plugin name — must match the name passed to Register-Plugin in the corresponding notification-*.ps1 file.

options.description: Plugin-specific options. Each plugin documents its own keys.

webhook_url.description: Webhook URL (used by discord and slack plugins).

token.description: Bearer token for authenticated topics (ntfy plugin).

Rule.description: A routing rule. When its conditions match, the listed providers are dispatched.

name.description: Human-readable rule name. Shown in agent logs when the rule fires.

match.description: 'all' = AND (every condition must pass). 'any' = OR (at least one condition must pass). Defaults to 'all' when omitted.

conditions.description: Conditions to evaluate against the task payload. Empty array [] matches every task (catch-all).

providers.description: Provider instance names to invoke when this rule matches. Must reference keys defined in the top-level 'providers' object.

stop_on_match.description: If true, stop evaluating further rules once this one matches (firewall-style). If false (default), continue evaluating all rules.

Condition.description: A single condition evaluated against a task payload field.

value.description: Value to compare against. Type must be appropriate for the field and operator: integer for numeric fields, boolean for flag fields, string for text fields, array for 'in'/'not_in' operators.

allOf.description: Boolean fields only support '=' and '!=' operators.
Numeric fields support all comparison operators.
'in' and 'not_in' operators require an array value.
'contains' and 'not_contains' operators are only valid for string fields.
